critic_raw:
The RNN input includes historical severity assessments — the same variable as the label. The model may learn to extrapolate recent severity values rather than model clinical trajectories. The comparison with logistic regression, which also receives severity history, tests extrapolation capacity rather than clinical insight.

Restricting to high-agreement clinicians introduces selection bias toward clearer cases, degrading generalizability to the general patient population.

Forcing equal stratification of severity-comorbidity combinations distorts the evaluation population relative to natural disease prevalence.
all_issues_raised: The RNN input sequence includes historical severity assessments — the same variable as the label — meaning the model may learn severity persistence/extrapolation rather than clinical trajectory modeling; the logistic regression baseline also uses severity history, so the comparison tests extrapolation capacity rather than clinical modeling.

Restricting to high-agreement clinicians for quality control introduces selection bias toward clearer cases, potentially degrading performance on the general patient population with ambiguous severity presentations.
Stratification forces equal representation of severity-comorbidity combinations that may not reflect natural disease prevalence, distorting the evaluation population relative to deployment reality.

round_transcripts.critic:
The RNN input includes historical severity assessments — the same variable as the label. The model may learn to extrapolate recent severity values rather than model clinical trajectories. The comparison with logistic regression, which also receives severity history, tests extrapolation capacity rather than clinical insight.

Restricting to high-agreement clinicians introduces selection bias toward clearer cases, degrading generalizability to the general patient population.

Forcing equal stratification of severity-comorbidity combinations distorts the evaluation population relative to natural disease prevalence.
round_transcripts.defender:
Using historical values of the outcome variable as input features is standard in autoregressive prediction tasks — longitudinal disease management explicitly aims to predict future severity from past severity trajectories. Both RNN and logistic regression receive the same historical severity features, so the comparison is fair. If the RNN learns better temporal extrapolation of severity, that is a genuine advantage for clinical monitoring.

High-agreement clinician restriction is a data quality requirement, not a selection bias source. Severity assessments with low inter-rater reliability are noisy labels that would degrade model training for both architectures. The fallback to consensus labels further mitigates this — consensus labels can be computed for all patients regardless of individual assessor agreement. The design presents ICC filtering as one option, not the exclusive approach.

Stratification by severity and comorbidity ensures that both model architectures train and are evaluated on populations that include severe and complex cases — without stratification, random sampling could produce training sets over-representing mild cases. The stratification produces a more stringent evaluation, not a distorted one.
